metadata.title:
WANT THE STATE TO CONTROL


ALL NATURAL RESOURCES

pages.raw_text:
/ should not go for improvements in
New. York or Ohio, which have
long ago disposed of their water
power, timber or coal beds that
once belonged to the people. The
revenues derived from the develop
ment of those resources helped to
make those states what they are,
and likewise Washington, Idaho
and Oregon need all that can be
gained from the development of
the natural resources within their
borders.


w. S. Yearsley, L. K. Armstrong
and Nj. Baumgartner were appoint
ed committee to tabulate the
water power, coal and other ma
tural resources of the northwest
and urge members of congress and
senators to uniting in having the
conservation or disposal of these
things placed in the hands of the
Estate government
